title: CSS Grid vs Flexbox Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with Grid and Flexbox.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in CSS?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using BOTH CSS Grid (for layout structure) and Flexbox (for alignment inside components) together.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using Flexbox compared to CSS Grid for a one-dimensional row of items (like a navbar)?
options: It makes your CSS files smaller and more readable.
It excels at flexibly aligning items in a single axis (row OR column).
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a TWO-DIMENSIONAL (rows AND columns) page layout?
options: Grid
Flexbox
Table
Float

questions.question: What is 'Content-First' development in the context of Flexbox?
options: The elements on the page determine how the layout looks as they grow and shrink.
The layout is defined first as a grid of rows and columns.
There is no difference.
You write the text before you write any CSS.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for combining both?
options: Only using Flexbox for everything.
Only using Grid for everything.
Using Grid for the overall page structure (the 'big pieces') and Flexbox inside those parts to align text and buttons.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
